commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] The remaining action is maintainer/security acceptance of the trusted HTML lifecycle, not a narrow automated repair.; Needs attention: No concrete exploit was found in the current patch, but the diff intentionally expands a local host-read HTML trust boundary and needs owner acceptance. (sha=4f4dccc48f26e4b4a3eda2b93e3f4df33f9d4c79)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds content-bound SQLite provenance for trusted generated HTML staged into outbound media, updates host-read validation and cleanup, and adds media/state regression coverage.

PR surface: Source +195, Tests +394, Generated +19. Total +608 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. Current main stages local reply media into media/outbound and Telegram reloads that staged .html through a host-read gate that only trusts temp-root realpaths; I did not run a live Telegram repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining action is maintainer/security acceptance of the trusted HTML lifecycle, not a narrow automated repair.

target.title: fix: allow trusted host-read html after outbound staging
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes Telegram/file outbound delivery for trusted generated `text/html` reports after the attachment is staged into the OpenClaw outbound media store.

PR #87982 allowed generated HTML reports under the OpenClaw temp root, but the live outbound flow loads the original file, copies it to `~/.openclaw/media/outbound/<uuid>.html`, then the channel adapter loads that staged copy again. The second load no longer lives under the temp root, so host-read rejected it and the Telegram message was silently dropped.

This extends the trusted-host-read HTML boundary to the outbound-staged copy through a **content-bound provenance marker** persisted in the shared SQLite state DB, instead of trusting the staging directory by path alone. Trust is bound to the exact staged bytes and verified read-once at host-read time.

Fixes #90557.

## Design

When `resolveOutboundAttachmentFromUrl` stages a file whose source was a trusted-generated HTML path (under the temp root), it records a provenance row in a new `outbound_media_provenance` table keyed by the staged file's realpath, storing the `sha256` + `size_bytes` of the staged bytes (`kind = "trusted-generated-html"`, `version = 1`).

At host-read time, a file inside `<mediaDir>/outbound/` is trusted **only if** a provenance row exists for its realpath **and** the row's `sha256` + size match the bytes that `loadWebMedia` already read. Files under the temp root keep their existing unconditional trust. The provenance row is pruned in lockstep with the staged file by `pruneStaleTrustedGeneratedHtmlMarkers`, wired into the media-store retention pass (`cleanOldMedia`).

Preserved guards (unchanged): regular file, not a symlink, `nlink === 1`, final read still validates text shape and HTML document shape.

## ClawSweeper P1 resolution

Two prior P1 code findings are addressed in this revision (independently confirmed sound by a second-model review, which also flagged the read-once requirement below):

- **Path-keyed provenance bypass** → **content-bound + read-once.** The marker now stores `sha256` + `size_bytes`, and host-read trust is decided by hashing the **same buffer `loadWebMedia` already read** (no second filesystem read, so there is no hash-then-read TOCTOU window). A file whose bytes are replaced after the marker is written is rejected. New regression test: stage bytes `B1` + mark with the real digest, overwrite the same path with different bytes `B2`, assert host-read is rejected with `path-not-allowed`.
- **Swallowed marker write failure** → **propagate.** `resolveOutboundAttachmentFromUrl` no longer swallows `markTrustedGeneratedHtmlPath` errors; on failure it best-effort unlinks the staged file and rethrows, so an infrastructure/write error surfaces through the normal media-failure path instead of deferring to a later silent Telegram reload drop. New test asserts the rethrow and the best-effort `rm`.

The trust expansion is therefore provenance-gated, content-bound, fail-closed, and persisted in SQLite (matching the canonical-storage policy; an in-memory allowlist would break delayed/restarted delivery).

## Test plan

```text
pnpm db:kysely:gen && pnpm db:kysely:check          # generated types in sync with the new table
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/media/web-media.test.ts src/media/outbound-attachment.test.ts
```

## Real behavior proof

### Deterministic real-code-path before/after (2026-06-24)

This exercises the **same production helpers the Telegram channel adapter uses** — `resolveOutboundAttachmentFromUrl` (stage) followed by `loadWebMedia(stagedPath, { hostReadCapability: true })` (the second reload) — on two worktrees that differ by **only this PR's diff**:

- **baseline** = `301213a05f` (this PR's merge-base with `main`; temp-root-only trust gate)
- **candidate** = `2dcfc944` (PR head; content-bound outbound provenance)

The driver writes a generated report under the real OpenClaw temp root, stages it into `media/outbound` (which moves it outside the temp root), then performs the host-read reload exactly as the channel adapter does. Environment: macOS (Darwin 25.4.0, arm64), Node v26.0.0.

```text
# baseline (301213a05f) — same source sha256 50fcd4b2…
[baseline] staged path   = …/media/outbound/pr90579-proof-…---<uuid>.html
[baseline] staged under media/outbound = true
[baseline] staged outside temp root    = true
[baseline] RESULT: REJECTED — staged reload host-read BLOCKED
[baseline]         LocalMediaAccessError: hostReadCapability permits only validated plain-text documents and trusted generated HTML reports for local reads

# candidate (2dcfc944) — identical source sha256 50fcd4b2…
[candidate] staged path   = …/media/outbound/pr90579-proof-…---<uuid>.html
[candidate] staged under media/outbound = true
[candidate] staged outside temp root    = true
[candidate] RESULT: DELIVERED — staged reload host-read ALLOWED (144 bytes, sha==source? true)
[candidate] TAMPER CHECK: ✅ replaced bytes REJECTED — LocalMediaAccessError: hostReadCapability permits only validated plain-text documents and trusted generated HTML reports for local reads
```

This is the exact production silent-drop (`LocalMediaAccessError: hostReadCapability permits only validated plain-text documents and trusted generated HTML reports for local reads`) on baseline, and delivery on candidate — with the live tamper step confirming the content binding rejects swapped bytes at the same staged path. It is deterministic, runs in seconds through the real `loadWebMedia` gate, and needs no Telegram client, Mantis SUT, or host-read SUT config. This is the staged-reload-with-host-read path the earlier Mantis run did not exercise (its SUT did not propagate `hostReadCapability`, so both lanes bypassed the gate).

### Original production trace + unit-level confirmation

Behavior addressed: when an agent reply emits `MEDIA:/tmp/openclaw/<file>.html`, OpenClaw's outbound pipeline loads the file once (`/tmp/openclaw/...` is inside the tmp root, so trusted), stages a copy to `~/.openclaw/media/outbound/<file>---<uuid>.html`, and then the Telegram channel adapter calls `loadWebMedia(stagedPath)` a second time. Before this patch the second call rejected because the staged path is no longer under `/tmp/openclaw/`, and the Telegram message was silently dropped. After this patch the staged path is trusted **iff** its bytes match the recorded provenance digest, and delivery proceeds.

Real environment tested: live OpenClaw on macOS 26.4 (Darwin 25.4.0, arm64), Node v26.0.0, Telegram Bot API channel (polling). The original silent-drop was reproduced 4 times on the unpatched build (#90557). For this PR the patched code path was exercised in the repository test harness, since the CI checkout cannot drive a live Telegram bot end-to-end.

Evidence after fix (PR head):

```text
node scripts/generate-kysely-types.mjs --verify
# (exit 0 — generated Kysely types match the schema, incl. sha256 / size_bytes columns)

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/media/web-media.test.ts src/media/outbound-attachment.test.ts
 ✓ media  src/media/web-media.test.ts (79 tests)
 ✓ media  src/media/outbound-attachment.test.ts (5 tests)
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  84 passed (84)
```

Key new coverage:

```text
# Replacement attack (A1): bytes swapped after the marker → rejected
✓ rejects an outbound-staged HTML whose bytes were replaced after the provenance marker (content-bound trust)
# Provenance row records the real sha256 + size_bytes (no hardcoded digest)
✓ writes a trusted-generated-html provenance row keyed by the staged file's realpath
# Marker write failure (A2): propagated + staged file unlinked
✓ propagates a marker write failure and best-effort unlinks the staged file
```

What was not tested from this checkout: live Telegram Bot API delivery, because the CI environment lacks bot credentials. The original silent-drop behavior was verified in production (#90557, 4 reproductions with a `console.error` probe in the installed `dist`); the proof here demonstrates the trusted-host-read gate now accepts the same staged path **only when its bytes match the recorded digest** and rejects a tampered or unmarked staged file. The same instrumented probe can be re-run on a build containing this PR to confirm end-to-end delivery if needed.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
